stories.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
stories.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

stories.title: The Israeli military wants several more weeks to fight Iran war, officials say
stories.description: The Israeli military estimates it would need several more weeks of fighting to complete its war goals in Iran, at a time when President Trump says the U.S. is negotiating an end to the war.

stories.title: Senate confirms Sen. Mullin as DHS secretary. And, Iran denies U.S. talks to end war
stories.description: The Senate has confirmed Markwayne Mullin as the next Department of Homeland Security secretary. And, Iran has denied that it's in talks with the U.S. to end the war, which is now in its fourth week.

stories.title: Trump declares victory and claims Iran offers a 'prize' in talks Iran has denied having
stories.description: President Trump said on Tuesday Iran made a valuable offer to reach a deal with the United States, in talks that Iran has denied are taking place. "We've won this. This war has been won," Trump said.

stories.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
stories.description: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

stories.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
stories.description: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

stories.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
stories.description: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Iranian missiles injure 180 in towns near Israeli nuclear site
stories.description: Israel is investigating how ballistic missiles got through the country's sophisticated air defences.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why there's no sign of the conflict in the Middle East 'winding down'
stories.description: Iran's foreign minister said that it doesn't appear that the US is prepared to halt its aggression after President Trump said the US was considering "winding down" its war.

stories.title: US lifts sanctions on some Iranian oil as energy prices soar
stories.description: The treasury secretary says the move will quickly bring about 140m barrels of oil to global markets.

stories.title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
stories.description: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

stories.title: UK allows US to use bases to strike Strait of Hormuz targets
stories.description: Downing Street says ministers approved the expansion after accusing Iran of "reckless strikes".

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: 'It's been bittersweet': The Iranian-Iraqi cafe drawing crowds to Hackney
stories.description: Logma, a newly opened Iranian-Iraqi cafe in Hackney, has gone viral for its sandwiches.

stories.title: Wary allies show there's no quick fix to Trump's Iran crisis
stories.description: European leaders are hesitant to help Trump secure the Strait of Hormuz, but they know inaction on the Iran war is not really an option.

stories.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
stories.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

stories.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
stories.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

stories.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
stories.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

stories.title: Middle East live: Israeli strikes on South Lebanon leave nine dead
stories.description: In Lebanon, Israeli strikes killed at least six people in a town and a Palestinian refugee camp in the southern Sidon area, and three more in another town. This comes as US President Donald Trump expresses optimism at ending the war, after sending a 15-point ceasefire plan to Ira...

stories.title: US proposes 15-point plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday he had sent a peace plan to Iran, expressing cautious optimism about ending nearly a month of war. In a potential sign of de-escalation, Tehran announced it would allow “non-hostile” vessels to pass through the strategically vital Strait of ...

stories.title: Iran war pushes plastic packaging costs higher
stories.description: Oil prices rose again on Tuesday, a day after a sharp decline, amid confusion over which way the Iran war was going. Brent crude settled well above 100 dollars a barrel. Meanwhile, rising energy costs stemming from the conflict are causing the cost of plastic packaging to skyrock...

stories.title: US to deploy elite army brigade to Middle East: reports
stories.description: The United States is planning to send some 3,000 soldiers from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East to support operations against Iran, US media reported Tuesday. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the Middle East by Friday, the latest deadline s...

stories.title: Ghana signs security partnership with the EU
stories.description: In tonight's edition, Ghana has become the first African country to enter into a landmark security and defence partnership with the European Union. Also, with several African countries heavily reliant on fertiliser imported from the Gulf, there's been a knock-on effect on the con...

stories.title: Pakistan’s prime minister says his nation is ready to facilitate talks to end Iran war
stories.description: Pakistan’s prime minister says his country is ready to “facilitate meaningful and conclusive talks” to end the Iran war. Pakistan is one of the countries pushing diplomatically for talks between Iran and the United States after President Donald Trump said Monday there were ongoin...

stories.title: Saudi leader said to be pushing Trump to continue war in Iran, New York Times reports
stories.description: The New York Times reports on Tuesday that Saudi Arabia’s de facto leader, Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, has been pushing President Trump to continue the war against Iran, arguing that the U.S.-Israeli military campaign presents a “historic opportunity” to remake the Middle E...

stories.title: Talks or theatre? Claims of peace talks to end Iran war collide with denials
stories.description: Are diplomatic talks to end the Iran conflict actually happening? Trump hinted at “major” progress, but the White House downplayed it. Iran says no, Qatar says they're not involved, while there's increasing speculation that Pakistan could be the host of talks later this week. Yet...

stories.title: War on Iran: 'Fought on faulty assumptions, notion of externally delivered regime change delusional'
stories.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Barbara Slavin, Author and Distinguished Fellow at the Stimson Center. According to Slavin, the war on Iran is not a coherent strategic endeavour, and reflects a pattern in which rhetoric trumps reality. Rather than emerging from necessity, dip...

stories.title: French economic growth undercut by war in Iran, national statistics office says
stories.description: The French economy will grow by only 0.2% in the first half of the year instead of the 0.3% forecast, the Insee national statistics agency said Tuesday, citing higher inflation linked to the war in Iran. Inflation will surpass 2% in coming months, rising sharply from the 1.1% rat...

stories.title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
stories.description: Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC commander, will manage Iran's security amid external US-Israeli pressures and internal unrest.

stories.title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
stories.description: Iran, Israel, the Gulf faced new missiles and drones. The US has signalled military escalation and openness to a deal.

stories.title: Amid US-Israeli attacks, people in Iran struggle to survive ailing economy
stories.description: Some are left without an income, others are temporarily displaced, and no one knows when the internet will return .

stories.title: As Iran war rocks global economy, Global South bears brunt of fallout
stories.description: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.

stories.title: Parsi: No deal ‘’without both sides giving something to the other’
stories.description: Trita Parsi argues that Iran is unlikely to agree to end the war without sanctions relief.

stories.title: New Zealand footballers willing to play Iran World Cup match outside US
stories.description: The teams are set to play on June 15 in Los Angeles, but Iran has threatened to boycott its matches due to the war.

stories.title: What we know about the US’s 15-point plan Iran proposal
stories.description: US media reports the Trump administration has proposed a temporary ceasefire and a 15-point plan to end the war on Iran.

stories.title: Iran says ‘non-hostile’ ships can pass safely through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Tehran's statement on opening of key waterway comes as US President Trump says talks are taking place to end the war.

stories.title: Philippine president declares energy emergency as impact of Iran war felt
stories.description: Transport unions say emergency declaration a 'superficial band-aid' that does not address root cause of fuel crisis.

stories.title: Iran war live: Iranian military dismisses Trump’s claim of negotiations
stories.description: US-Israeli strikes on Iran continue despite claims of talks, with at least 12 killed, 28 wounded in an attack in Tehran.

stories.title: Trump keeps up claims of talks with ‘the right people’ in Iran
stories.description: US reportedly engaged in backchannel efforts, though Israel apparently not on same page, and military buildup continues.

stories.title: Explosion, fire at Erbil high-rise amid Iranian attacks
stories.description: Video shows a large explosion and fire at a high-rise building in Erbil reportedly near the US Consulate.

stories.title: Israel’s army strikes two missile production sites in Tehran
stories.description: According to the IDF, the two Iranian plants operated under the command of Iran’s Ministry of Defense

stories.title: No plans to scale down Iran operation during talks, White House says
stories.description: Acording to the report, the US had sent Iran a 15-point plan to end the war

stories.title: Britain expected quick US victory in Iran — Russian ambassador Kelin
stories.description: The official said that in spite of the irritation of US President Donald Trump over London's reluctance to participate in unblocking the Strait of Hormuz, the depth of the transatlantic differences shall not be exagerated

stories.title: Iran suspects trick in US offer for talks — Axios
stories.description: Sources cited by the news portal say that the deployment of major troop reinforcements by the US has strengthened the suspicions in Tehran

stories.title: At least 12 people killed in attack on Iranian city near Tehran
stories.description: 28 people were injured during the attack

stories.title: Iran calls for creation of regional military alliance without United States
stories.description: Iran’s General Staff says that the countries of the region "will never need a state located thousands of kilometers away to guarantee their security"

stories.title: About 290 US troops wounded since beginning of operation against Iran — CNN
stories.description: According to a source in the Central Command, of the affected soldiers, 255 returned to service

stories.title: Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed only to ships of hostile countries
stories.description: The Islamic republic noted that the full restoration of security and sustainable stability in the strait depends on the cessation of hostilities by the US and Israel

stories.title: Britain works with partners to open Strait of Hormuz — Starmer
stories.description: The UK prime minister expressed "unwavering support" for Saudi Arabia

stories.title: US hands 15-point proposal to end war over to Iran — NYT
stories.description: According to sources cited by the newspaper, the plan affects the missile and nuclear programs of the Islamic republic

stories.title: Iran’s foreign minister appreciates German president's statement on US-Israeli attacks
stories.description: Abbas Araghchi says Frank-Walter Steinmeier "should be given credit" for condemning the violations against Iranians

stories.title: No talks between Iran and Washington during US aggression — Iran’s ambassador to Russia
stories.description: Kazem Jalali says there were no negotiations between representatives of the countries for more than 20 days, the entire duration of the conflict

stories.title: Trump threatens Iran with destruction of $10 billion power plant
stories.description: There are no obstacles for the US to strike targets in Iran, the president says

stories.title: Israel strikes Iran’s largest explosives production facility in Isfahan
stories.description: Besides, the Israeli Air Force attacked "dozens" of Iranian facilities during the day, including ballistic missile launching sites, weapons production sites, and air defense systems

stories.title: Iran agrees not to enrich uranium — US leader
stories.description: Donald Trump added that the Islamic republic "can’t have certain things"

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
